default_title: Interop Strategy — Dragon Copilot & Scribe-CDS Convergence
landing_intro: Meridian sits at the convergence of ambient AI scribes, EHR-native CDS, and third-party clinical decision support. Dragon Copilot (100K+ clinicians), Abridge CRE (Kaiser 25K physicians), and Epic-native AI (160–200 projects) are all moving toward patient-specific action lists. This module maps the threat picture, five durable differentiators, and a phased integration posture that keeps Meridian relevant without coupling to any single platform.

checklist.statement: CDS Hooks service live — /cds-services/meridian-manifest implements HL7 CDS Hooks 2.0 patient-view with FHIR prefetch consumption
SMART on FHIR launch ready — PKCE OAuth2 handshake, Patient/Condition/MedicationRequest/Observation fetch, read-only posture
Dragon Copilot agent stub created — Copilot Studio manifest + OpenAPI schema in dragon-agent/; deployment not yet executed
Scribe-downstream positioning defined — "Meridian-after-Abridge" framing; /api/modules/match is the callable contract for any scribe partner

green_zone.zone_label: Phase A mostly implemented — interop layer is callable
green_zone.narrative_html: The EHR-agnostic interop layer (Phase A) is built: trigger schema in modules/index.json, /api/modules/match endpoint, CDS Hooks service, and SMART on FHIR launch. The Dragon agent stub (Phase B) exists but is not deployed. Scribe partnerships (Phase C) require no additional build — only outreach. Five durable differentiators guide every feature decision: push-based manifest, per-provider authoring, cross-EHR, scribe-downstream, and citation-traceable.

escalation.statement: Dragon Copilot threat — 100K+ clinicians, Information Assist integrates OpenEvidence natively; 20–50% real-world adoption ceiling is the counterweight
Abridge CRE threat — Kaiser 25K physicians, Best in KLAS 2025–2026, emits structured problems/orders from ambient audio; simultaneously highest-value partnership target
Epic AI commoditization — Art + Cosmos + CoMET + Agent Factory will own "AI-native CDS for Epic shops" by 2027; Meridian's lane is cross-EHR and non-interruptive
Auth not implemented — CORS is allow_origins=["*"], no Bearer/Entra scheme on /api/modules/match or /cds-services/*
No partner outreach yet — OpenEvidence (cheapest), Microsoft (highest-leverage), Abridge (highest-stakes/Kaiser overlap) all uncontacted
Dragon agent not registered — manifest exists but not submitted to Healthcare Agent Service; 3–6 month marketplace listing cycle
FHIR lab code coverage incomplete — current SMART_LAB_CODE_MAP covers 6 labs; full module matching needs uACR, creatinine, potassium, phosphorus, PTH, ferritin, etc.
Strategy invalidation risk — if Dragon opens Information Assist to customer-authored knowledge sources with per-provider ranking, differentiator 3.2 collapses
Strategy invalidation risk — if Epic ships a provider-customizable pre-visit manifest inside Art, differentiator 3.1 collapses

Anti-goals enforced — no free-form unbounded chatbot (Meridian Coach v1 is the bounded form: refuses clinical recommendations, routes to modules), don't pursue Agent Factory, don't Epic-couple, don't clone Navina HCC/coding, don't match Dragon on content breadth

context_strip.label: Build state
context_strip.text: Phase A implemented (trigger schema, /api/modules/match, CDS Hooks, SMART launch). Meridian Coach v1 shipped (Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 behind /api/agent/message, same contract as FastAPI). Phase B stubbed (dragon-agent/ directory). Phase C requires outreach only. Last updated 2026-04-22.

footer_note: Source: docs/INTEROP_STRATEGY.md — April 2026. Full document includes 50+ primary sources, vendor deployment counts, and HIMSS 2026 announcements.

faqs.title: Who are the three major threats?

items.question: What is Dragon Copilot's current state?
items.answer_html:
GA March 2025. Folds Dragon Medical One (dictation) + DAX Copilot (ambient) into one product. HIMSS 2026 re-positioning: "agentic clinical assistant." >100,000 clinicians, 600+ orgs, 58 languages.

Information Assist pulls from UpToDate, OpenEvidence, Elsevier ClinicalKey, CDC, FDA, MedlinePlus. Produces conversational orders, note summaries, referral letters.

Extensibility is the strategic opening: Partner REST APIs, DAXKit SDK, Healthcare Agent Service in Copilot Studio, three temporal hooks (pre/during/post encounter), 17 launch partners including OpenEvidence and Optum.

Counterweight: real-world adoption is 20–50%. KLAS Best in Ambient AI went to Abridge in 2025 and 2026, not Dragon.

Dragon Copilot analysis on GitHub

items.question: Why is Abridge both a threat and a partnership target?
items.answer_html:
Abridge CRE (Feb 2025, post $250M Series D) emits structured problems, predicted orders, and care gaps from ambient audio. First "Pal" in Epic's Workshop program. Largest ambient deployment: Kaiser Permanente, 40 hospitals, 600 offices, 25K physicians.

Threat: Abridge CDS is explicitly "closing care gaps at the point of conversation" — Meridian's core territory.

Partnership: A Meridian module triggered from an Abridge conversation is a wedge Epic-native CDS cannot close (because Epic's native scribe competes with Abridge). This is the "Meridian-after-Abridge" positioning.

Abridge analysis on GitHub

items.question: How does Epic-native AI affect the landscape?
items.answer_html:
160–200 active AI projects. >85% of Epic's customer base uses at least one Epic AI feature. Key surfaces:

Art — ambient charting + conversational Q&A + Cosmos "similar patients."
CoMET — generative medical-event transformer trained on 300M patients.
Agent Factory — no-code agent builder. Customer-local, not an ISV channel.

Epic is closing Workshop → Toolbox/Showroom. BPA override rates (49–96%) are Meridian's marketing moat: "the CDS that doesn't interrupt you."

Epic AI analysis on GitHub

faqs.title: What are the five durable differentiators?

items.question: What are the five differentiators?
items.answer_html:
Every feature decision should ladder to one of these:

3.1 Push-based, visit-start manifest — fires before the encounter opens. Dragon is ambient/pull; Abridge triggers on conversation; Epic BPAs fire late. Hard Stops panel is P0.

3.2 Per-provider / per-org authoring — clinician-authored decision trees. DOCX→JSON pipeline + triggers in index.json. Neither Dragon nor Epic lets a UC medical director author their own manifest.

3.3 Cross-EHR / EHR-agnostic — works with Oracle, Meditech, Athena, eCW. Epic-native tools do not. Do not Epic-couple the roadmap.

3.4 Scribe-downstream, not scribe-competing — Meridian becomes the module the scribe's structured output opens. /api/modules/match is the callable contract.

3.5 Citation-traceable, FDA non-device CDS — every recommendation is source-reviewable. Meridian Coach v1 is the live exemplar: the soft-steer pattern refuses to generate clinical recommendations, routes clinical questions to the relevant module (which the clinician reviews in full), and redirects pure-clinical questions to OpenEvidence. This preserves criterion #4 by construction, not by disclaimer.

Differentiators on GitHub

items.question: What are the anti-goals?
items.answer_html:
Things we should not do:

Coach v1 is the bounded shape of the chatbot — not the anti-goal. It refuses to generate clinical recommendations, routes to modules, redirects to OpenEvidence. This is the deliberate answer to the "don't ship a free-form chatbot" rule: ship a tightly-scoped one.

No free-form chatbot that generates clinical recommendations — would violate FDA criterion #4 and directly compete with Dragon Information Assist without advantage. Coach must never drift here.
Do not pursue Agent Factory — customer-local, not a third-party channel.
Do not depend on Workshop — being deprecated.
Don't clone Navina HCC/coding — that quadrant is owned (3M patients, KLAS #1).
Don't retrofit PHI onto Cloudflare Pages — BAA gap blocks enterprise.
Do not Epic-couple — cross-EHR is differentiator #3.
Don't match Dragon on breadth — win on authorship, structure, and push-timing.

faqs.title: What is the Phase A interop layer?

items.question: What was built in Phase A?
items.answer_html:
Four components, all implemented 2026-04-18:

Trigger schema — machine-readable triggers in modules/index.json (problem keywords, med regex, lab thresholds, priority). Single source of truth for frontend panel, match endpoint, and CDS Hooks service.

/api/modules/match — POST with patient context JSON or GET with ?mrn=. Returns ranked [{module_id, title, reason, priority, category}] + OE pre-prompt.

CDS Hooks service — /cds-services/meridian-manifest, HL7 2.0 patient-view handler. Consumes FHIR prefetch bundles, returns cards with deep-links.

SMART on FHIR launch — PKCE OAuth2, Patient/Condition/MedicationRequest/Observation fetch, FHIR→Meridian shape mapping. Read-only by design.

Phase A details on GitHub

faqs.title: What is the Dragon Copilot agent?

items.question: What does the Dragon agent do?
items.answer_html:
Wraps Phase A and turns Meridian into a Dragon Copilot-resident card surface. Two skills:

pre_encounter_manifest(patient) — POSTs to /api/modules/match; returns the visit-start manifest as cards Dragon renders in its pre-encounter hook.

open_module(module_id, patient_id) — returns a deep-link URL Dragon surfaces as a button.

Registration path: Healthcare Agent Service in Copilot Studio → tenant-admin install at Optum/CRH → Microsoft commercial marketplace listing. 3–6 months from build to marketplace listing, gated on Phase A backend standing up on BAA-covered infrastructure.

Dragon agent README on GitHub

faqs.topic: Phase C — Scribe Partnerships & Open Gaps
faqs.title: How does scribe-partner integration work, and what remains unresolved?

items.question: Which scribe partners should be pursued?
items.answer_html:
Once the CDS Hooks service and /api/modules/match are externally available, scribes can call Meridian post-encounter. Priority order:

Abridge — highest-value target because of Kaiser overlap. Partnership conversation, not a build task.

Ambience / Suki / DeepScribe / Heidi / Glass — same mechanism. Heidi's community prompt marketplace is a distribution-channel model worth studying. Glass Health is the closest functional competitor (ambient + native CDS + differential dx).

OpenEvidence — already the deep-link target; with BAAs now free (April 2025), cultivate before Dragon's embedded OE consumption erodes the relationship.

items.question: What is the 'Meridian-after-Abridge' positioning?
items.answer_html:
If Abridge captures a conversation and emits structured problems/orders, Meridian becomes the module those signals open. This is the "Meridian-after-Abridge" wedge — and it's a position Epic-native CDS cannot close, because Epic's native scribe competes with Abridge.

The /api/modules/match contract is the mechanism: POST patient context (from any source), get back ranked modules + deep-links. No Abridge-specific build required — just a partnership conversation.

Scribe partnership details on GitHub

items.question: What are the key open questions across the interop surface?
items.answer_html:
Auth design — CORS is *, no Bearer scheme. Static API key? OAuth2 client credentials? Tenant-scoped JWT? Decision needed before PHI flows.

Trigger schema versioning — if external services depend on the match contract, schema changes need versioning discipline. Not yet defined.

FHIR lab code coverage — 6 labs mapped; full module matching needs uACR, creatinine, potassium, phosphorus, PTH, ferritin, B12, folate, TSH.

Hard stops in match response — currently returns modules + priority + reason but not hard stops. Should aggregate them for Dragon agent cards.

Partner outreach sequencing — OpenEvidence (cheapest), Microsoft (highest-leverage, longest cycle), Abridge (highest-stakes, Kaiser overlap). Which first?

Open questions on GitHub

faqs.title: How does Meridian Coach fit into the interop story?

items.question: Is Meridian Coach the "AI-native CDS" threat to Meridian itself?
items.answer_html: No — it's the bounded, first-party answer to that question. Coach v1 deliberately does not compete with Dragon Information Assist or Epic Art. It doesn't answer clinical questions; it navigates users to Meridian modules and coaches them on using the modules themselves. That keeps the moat (physician-authored content + push-based manifest + cross-EHR) intact and adds the one user-facing surface that new users consistently ask for.

items.question: What needs to happen for Coach to work with Dragon/Abridge?
items.answer_html:
Nothing today — Coach lives entirely inside Meridian's own UI. For Phase 2+ scenarios where a scribe surface wants to embed Coach's Q&A capability, the same /api/agent/message SSE contract can be called from a Dragon Copilot Studio skill or an Abridge embed. Same backend, same tools, different frontend.

Alternative path: a Copilot Studio agent that implements the Coach contract natively — tighter M365 integration (SSO, Purview audit, Teams surfacing) at the cost of model-choice control. Credible Phase 2 option if enterprise demands it.

items.question: What about future sub-agents and literature verification?
items.answer_html:
Sub-agents per subject area become worth building when the module registry outgrows a single generalist prompt (roughly 30–50 modules). A lightweight router triages the user's question and hands off to the right specialist. Not needed yet at ~15 modules.

Literature-verification agents are a separate, background workstream: periodic checks of each module against OpenEvidence/PubMed/guidelines, surfacing suggested updates when evidence shifts. Dr. Varun Malik has already built agents that do exactly this in his own work — natural starting point for reuse or design inspiration. Keeps Meridian modules current without manual re-review cadence.
